common_definition_of_done: Implementation is complete with no placeholders, TODO-only paths or fabricated outputs.
Applicable unit, integration, system, regression and real-fixture tests pass.
Changed-code branch coverage is at least 90%; critical gates and invariants are 100%.
No skipped, xfailed, flaky, unexecuted, stale or unverifiable result is treated as PASS.
Every command is submitted through the simplicio-loop Hub; no local scheduler, queue or process pool is introduced.
Evidence records run/task/attempt/source SHA, policy hash, tool versions, command, duration, environment, seed and artifact hashes.
Unavailable metrics are null plus a machine-readable reason, never zero or an estimate.
Documentation and changelog are updated and the final commit is green.

issues.title: Define and enforce the quality-extension architecture boundary
issues.problem: An extension can accidentally duplicate Loop orchestration and split authority.
steps: Write the core-versus-extension ADR and ownership matrix.
Define the only allowed Loop execution port.
Prohibit local scheduler, queue, worker pool, worktree, lease, retry and terminal-state modules.
Add import and filesystem architecture checks.
Document blocked behavior when the Loop or Hub is unavailable.
acceptance: Every quality execution crosses the Loop port.
No duplicate orchestration component exists.
The Completion Oracle remains the sole terminal authority.
tests: Architecture test for forbidden modules/imports.
Trace proving a quality command is delegated to the Loop.

issues.title: Bootstrap the installable package, CLI and repository governance
issues.problem: The project needs a clean, reproducible base before agents can be implemented.
steps: Create src-layout Python package and console scripts.
Add license, README, AGENTS contract, contribution and security policy.
Add build, lint, type, unit and clean-install checks.
Ship the loop-extension manifest and packaged contracts.
Create PR/issue templates and changelog policy.
acceptance: Source install, wheel install, --help and --version work in empty environments.
Package contains contracts and no repository-only imports.
Initial CI/local gate is green.
tests: Wheel/sdist content inspection.
Clean-venv CLI smoke on supported Python versions.

issues.title: Implement strict, versioned quality policy as code
issues.problem: Manual checklists cannot enforce required lanes, thresholds or waivers.
steps: Define quality-policy/v1 schema.
Model risk levels, mandatory lanes, coverage and performance budgets.
Implement global/project/CLI precedence without silent fields.
Hash the resolved policy and bind it to every receipt.
Fail closed on invalid, ambiguous or unknown configuration.
acceptance: Resolution is deterministic and auditable.
Critical risk can only add requirements, never remove them.
Invalid configuration blocks before work submission.
tests: Golden precedence fixtures.
Parser property/fuzz tests.
Unknown-field and boundary-value corpus.

issues.title: Define canonical quality plan, evidence, finding and verdict schemas
issues.problem: Agents need closed, versioned contracts that distinguish missing, zero, null, skipped and N/A.
steps: Model quality plan, stage request/result, evidence item, finding, waiver and gate verdict.
Include run/task/attempt/source/diff/policy/toolchain identity.
Define metric value, unit, samples and unavailable reason.
Close every schema against unknown fields.
Add migrations and compatibility fixtures.
acceptance: Schemas validate independently of Python code.
Unavailable metrics are null plus reason, never zero.
All status and binding semantics are explicit.
tests: Valid/invalid golden corpus.
Round-trip and migration tests.
Schema mutation and fuzz tests.

issues.title: Create quality-agent and test-tool adapter contracts
issues.problem: Languages and tools must plug in without changing the core or spawning processes directly.
steps: Define agent and adapter protocols.
Declare capabilities, supported versions, resource requests and output formats.
Require commands to be ProcessSpec-like data, never executed by adapters.
Define failure taxonomy and cancellation semantics.
Publish conformance fixtures for valid, incompatible and hostile plugins.
acceptance: Incompatible plugins fail before execution.
Plugins cannot obtain scheduling or terminal authority.
Capability discovery is deterministic.
tests: Fake sync/async/crashing plugins.
Malicious plugin and unknown capability tests.

issues.title: Build the QualityAgent SDK and conformance suite
issues.problem: All specialized agents need one auditable lifecycle and evidence API.
steps: Implement agent context, plan, stage request, result and evidence emitter.
Propagate cancellation/deadline without owning retries.
Enforce author/auditor independence metadata.
Provide deterministic seeds and idempotency keys.
Run a shared conformance suite against every agent.
acceptance: Same inputs and seed produce the same plan.
Agents never schedule, close issues or merge.
Every result is commit- and policy-bound.
tests: Conformance tests for pass/fail/block/cancel/crash.
Identity and self-approval negative tests.

issues.title: Implement Loop capability and version negotiation
issues.problem: The extension must not assume core APIs or silently downgrade.
steps: Probe core version and extension contract.
Probe stage agents, Hub IPC, ProcessSpec, ledger, quality hook and oracle capabilities.
Maintain N-1/N/current compatibility fixtures.
Emit actionable missing-capability reason codes.
Forbid local fallback in strict mode.
acceptance: Unsupported core blocks before mutation.
Doctor reports exact versions and capabilities.
Future unknown versions do not silently pass.
tests: Supported, N-1, future and partially-capable Loop fixtures.

issues.title: Implement the centralized Hub test-process adapter
issues.problem: Test tools must not create decentralized subprocesses.
steps: Translate tool requests into Loop ProcessSpec data.
Submit, poll, collect and cancel through the Hub client.
Propagate priority=test, quotas, cwd, environment allowlist and output limits.
Normalize ProcessResult without losing raw evidence.
Fail closed on Hub loss or incompatible result.
acceptance: No runtime adapter imports subprocess.
Every command has Loop process/run/stage IDs.
Timeout and cancellation produce terminal receipts.
tests: Fake and real Hub integration.
Timeout, crash, truncation, reconnect and cancellation.

issues.title: Compile quality plans into Loop stage graphs
issues.problem: Agent dependencies and waves must be owned by the Loop.
steps: Map lanes to role bindings and stage definitions.
Compile a deterministic DAG with resource classes.
Validate cycles, duplicate IDs and missing capabilities.
Submit the graph through the public extension registry.
Persist graph hash and policy hash.
acceptance: The extension only supplies a graph; Loop plans waves.
Equivalent plans produce byte-identical graph hashes.
Invalid graphs block without partial execution.
tests: Golden graphs for fan-in/fan-out.
Cycle and capability failure fixtures.

issues.title: Integrate the Loop stage journal and idempotent quality events
issues.problem: Reconnect, replay or duplicate delivery must not rerun tests or corrupt evidence.
steps: Use the Loop StageCoordinatorJournal as the only event journal.
Create deterministic correlation and idempotency keys.
Handle duplicate, late and out-of-order events.
Recover accepted stage receipts after restart.
Record invalidation events without deleting history.
acceptance: Replay is side-effect free.
Accepted evidence survives restart when still fresh.
Corruption blocks with a reason code.
tests: Crash/restart, duplicate and reordered event tests.
Journal tamper fixture.

issues.title: Map quality failures, deadlines, cancellation and retryability
issues.problem: Quality must classify failures without implementing its own retry engine.
steps: Define permanent/transient/cancelled/resource/policy failure codes.
Propagate deadlines and cancellation tokens.
Return retry hints, never execute retries locally.
Map exhausted attempts to FAIL or BLOCKED.
Preserve first and final failure evidence.
acceptance: Loop remains the only retry authority.
Cancellation reaches all submitted work.
Retry exhaustion never becomes partial PASS.
tests: Timeout, rate-limit, OOM, cancellation and retry-exhaustion fixtures.

issues.title: Wire the required quality lifecycle hook in fail-closed mode
issues.problem: A run can otherwise proceed from implementation to verify without full quality.
steps: Register the provider with the Loop hook.
Run quality after implementation and before watcher/delivery/oracle.
Persist hook input and provider output atomically without deciding the verdict.
Support required, plan-only and explicitly non-terminal diagnostic modes.
Block on missing provider, partial write, crash or invalid result.
acceptance: Required quality cannot be bypassed.
No extension-local done state exists.
Lifecycle plumbing cannot manufacture PASS; audit and gate remain downstream.
tests: End-to-end PASS/FAIL/BLOCKED runs.
Missing hook, crash and partial-file regression tests.

issues.title: Build repository and changed-surface impact analysis
issues.problem: The suite must adapt to any repository without guessing affected surfaces.
steps: Detect languages, build systems, public APIs and existing test infrastructure.
Resolve changed files, renames, deletes and generated files.
Use project-map.json when available and record its freshness.
Compute dependencies, reverse dependents and risk surfaces.
Represent unknown facts explicitly.
acceptance: Produces a confidence-scored impact map.
Mapper absence degrades visibly, never fabricates context.
Polyglot and monorepo boundaries are preserved.
tests: Polyglot monorepo, rename/delete and stale-map fixtures.

issues.title: Classify risk and select mandatory quality suites monotonically
issues.problem: Required lanes must reflect real risk without allowing critical changes to weaken gates.
steps: Score API, security, data, concurrency, performance, packaging and blast radius.
Map risk classes to mandatory lanes and budgets.
Require a waiver for any exclusion.
Make risk monotonic when impact increases.
Persist explanations and inputs.
acceptance: Same impact yields same classification.
Critical risk includes every lower-risk requirement.
Overrides cannot silently lower risk.
tests: Golden risk table.
Property tests for monotonicity.
Adversarial override tests.

issues.title: Map acceptance criteria to tests and evidence
issues.problem: A green suite is insufficient when acceptance criteria remain unproven.
steps: Assign stable IDs to acceptance criteria.
Link each criterion to planned tests and evidence types.
Detect orphan criteria, ambiguous duplicates and irrelevant tests.
Persist AC→test→evidence traceability.
Fail the gate when required criteria lack accepted evidence.
acceptance: Every required AC has at least one independent proof.
Traceability survives retries and renames.
Ambiguity blocks instead of guessing.
tests: Markdown/JSON issue fixtures.
Duplicate, missing and contradictory AC cases.

issues.title: Implement the stateless QualityPlannerAgent
issues.problem: A specialized agent must build the quality plan without becoming a scheduler.
steps: Consume policy, impact and AC traceability.
Select agents, lanes, dependencies and resources.
Explain inclusion, N/A candidates and blockers.
Emit one deterministic quality-plan receipt.
Submit the graph to Loop and stop planning.
acceptance: Agent contains no queue or scheduling logic.
Low/high/critical plans are reproducible.
Every lane has rationale and evidence target.
tests: Golden plans and repeatability tests.
Architecture import checks.

issues.title: Implement content-addressed evidence and reproducibility manifests
issues.problem: PASS must point to verifiable artifacts, commands and environments.
steps: Define evidence items and SHA-256 storage.
Record source, policy, tool versions, command spec, environment and seed.
Redact secrets before persistence.
Deduplicate identical artifacts without losing provenance.
Define retention and missing-artifact behavior.
acceptance: Tampering invalidates the receipt.
Every PASS resolves to present hashed evidence.
Secret material is not persisted.
tests: Tamper, dedup, redaction, missing artifact and replay tests.

issues.title: Govern applicability, NOT_APPLICABLE and waivers
issues.problem: Skip, unavailable and structurally irrelevant must not be conflated.
steps: Define reason codes and allowed scopes.
Require justification, independent approver and expiration.
Bind waiver to policy, source and lane.
Reject autoapproval and broad wildcard waivers.
Expose all waivers in terminal reports.
acceptance: Skipped/timeout/unavailable never equal N/A.
Expired or stale waivers block.
Producer cannot approve its own waiver.
tests: Valid, expired, self-approved, stale and overbroad waiver cases.

issues.title: Implement the independent EvidenceAuditAgent
issues.problem: Producer claims need independent re-execution and provenance checks before any gate can pass.
steps: Enforce identity separation between author, executor and auditor.
Rehash artifacts and revalidate run, task, attempt, fence, source and policy bindings.
Re-run selected deterministic commands through Hub.
Cross-check thresholds, raw results and environment fingerprints.
Emit a content-addressed audit receipt and blocking contradictions.
acceptance: Producer cannot audit its own evidence.
Forged, stale, cross-run or cross-commit evidence is rejected.
The audit receipt is required input to the deterministic gate.
tests: Forged, missing, stale, cross-run and cross-attempt fixtures.
Independent rerun mismatch, tamper and identity-separation tests.

issues.title: Implement the deterministic QualityGateAgent
issues.problem: The terminal quality decision must be recomputed only after independent audit.
steps: Load resolved policy, fresh receipts and the independent audit receipt.
Validate lane states, thresholds, bindings, findings and waivers.
Reject contradictions, missing evidence, stale SHA and failed audit.
Emit one deterministic and explainable gate verdict.
Project only the accepted verdict to the Loop quality matrix.
acceptance: Same audited input produces the same verdict.
Any missing mandatory proof or audit receipt fails closed.
Gate agent cannot close, merge, execute tests or self-audit.
tests: Decision-table and missing-audit tests.
Mutation tests of every failure branch.
Oracle projection integration.

issues.title: Implement findings, remediation requests and re-verification
issues.problem: A failed test must return to Loop recovery without a quality-local fix loop.
steps: Normalize and fingerprint findings.
Set severity, owner stage and affected evidence.
Submit a correction request to Loop recovery.
Invalidate dependent receipts.
Re-run the failed lane and required regression lanes after correction.
acceptance: Finding closes only after fresh proof.
Duplicates collapse without losing occurrences.
Budget exhaustion terminates BLOCKED.
tests: Corrected, recurring, duplicate, nonreproducible and exhausted-budget cases.

issues.title: Specify hermetic test environments and cleanup
issues.problem: Agents need reproducible services, fixtures and cleanup without provisioning locally.
steps: Model services, ports, filesystem, network, credentials, seeds and cleanup.
Submit provisioning requirements to Loop.
Verify isolation and resource ownership.
Capture environment fingerprints.
Block when cleanup cannot be verified.
acceptance: Runs are reproducible and isolated.
No leaked process, file, connection or service remains.
Offline restrictions are honored.
tests: Real service fixture, cleanup failure and offline execution.

issues.title: Implement StaticCodeQualityAgent
issues.problem: Lint, formatting, typing, complexity, duplication and dead code need normalized evidence.
steps: Discover available analyzers.
Build Hub commands and version pins.
Normalize findings and SARIF.
Apply policy thresholds.
Distinguish tool absence from clean code.
acceptance: Required analyzer absence blocks or needs waiver.
Findings map to source and rule IDs.
No formatter silently mutates product code.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Seeded lint/type/complexity defects and SARIF golden files.

issues.title: Implement UnitTestAgent
issues.problem: Changed logic needs isolated behavioral tests and negative paths.
steps: Discover unit suites and framework.
Identify changed units and missing tests.
Submit test-authoring tasks when authorized.
Execute unit tests through Hub.
Emit per-test result, duration and evidence.
acceptance: Missing required unit coverage fails.
Empty/always-pass/mock-only tests are rejected.
Negative paths are represented.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Pass/fail/timeout/empty-test fixtures.

issues.title: Implement ComponentTestAgent
issues.problem: Whole modules need boundary validation beyond isolated units.
steps: Define component boundaries from impact map.
Provision controlled collaborators.
Execute module-level flows.
Capture state and cleanup evidence.
Classify overlap with unit/integration without double counting.
acceptance: Component tests exercise real module boundaries.
State leakage blocks.
Evidence is distinct from unit-only proof.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Filesystem, cache and adapter component fixtures.

issues.title: Implement IntegrationTestAgent
issues.problem: Database, queue, filesystem and API seams must be exercised with real collaborators.
steps: Discover integration dependencies.
Request real disposable services from Loop.
Seed deterministic data.
Execute success and failure paths.
Verify cleanup and idempotency.
acceptance: Mock-only seams do not satisfy mandatory integration.
Unavailable required service blocks.
Real collaborator versions are recorded.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Database, queue, API and filesystem fixtures.

issues.title: Implement ContractTestAgent
issues.problem: APIs, CLIs, schemas and events can break consumers while internal tests stay green.
steps: Discover provider/consumer contracts.
Version and snapshot public shapes.
Execute compatibility checks.
Detect unversioned breaking changes.
Emit consumer/provider evidence.
acceptance: Breaking change without approved versioning blocks.
CLI exit/output and event schemas are covered.
Backward compatibility policy is explicit.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Compatible and breaking API/CLI/event fixtures.

issues.title: Implement SystemE2EAgent
issues.problem: User-facing behavior must be tested through the actual command/API/UI surface.
steps: Resolve the real entrypoint.
Provision a clean environment.
Execute happy, error, timeout, cancel and recovery flows.
Capture observable output and side effects.
Tear down and audit resources.
acceptance: Tests do not call internal functions as a substitute.
At least one real end-to-end path proves each critical AC.
Runtime errors and console/log errors block.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Clean-room full-flow fixtures.

issues.title: Implement RegressionTestAgent with fail-before/pass-after proof
issues.problem: A fixed bug can return unless its original failure is permanently reproduced.
steps: Extract or author the minimal reproducer.
Capture failing pre-fix evidence when possible.
Run the same case against the fixed source.
Run related existing suites.
Verify reverting the fix makes the regression fail.
acceptance: Bug issues require a specific reproducer.
RED and GREEN are distinct and commit-bound.
A generic full-suite pass is insufficient.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Seeded bug, revert and stale-RED fixtures.

issues.title: Implement packaged-artifact and real-code smoke testing
issues.problem: Source-checkout tests can hide broken packages, imports and entrypoints.
steps: Build the official artifact once.
Install it in an empty environment without repository paths.
Verify version, imports, commands and minimal configuration.
Exercise upgrade/reinstall/uninstall where supported.
Record artifact digest and installed file origin.
acceptance: No source-tree import satisfies real-code proof.
Exact built artifact is tested.
Broken entrypoint or metadata blocks.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Wheel/npm/crate/container clean-install fixtures as applicable.

issues.title: Implement line, branch, condition, changed and critical coverage
issues.problem: Line coverage alone can hide untested decisions and changed code.
steps: Discover native coverage format.
Normalize paths across worktrees/containers.
Collect line, branch/condition and diff coverage.
Mark critical gates/invariants.
Compare against policy and previous baseline.
acceptance: Defaults enforce global >=85, changed >=90 and critical 100.
Missing coverage is null plus reason and blocks when required.
Coverage cannot decrease silently.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Multiple report formats, path remap and threshold boundary tests.

issues.title: Implement PropertyFuzzAgent with reproducible minimization
issues.problem: Example tests miss broad input spaces and malformed data.
steps: Derive explicit properties and generators.
Set deterministic seeds and budgets.
Run property and fuzz engines through Hub.
Persist and minimize failing corpus.
Promote stable regressions into normal tests.
acceptance: Every failure is replayable from seed/corpus.
Timeout is not treated as success.
Minimized cases retain the failure.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Seeded defect found and minimized.

issues.title: Implement MutationTestAgent and strength thresholds
issues.problem: High coverage can still come from tests that assert nothing useful.
steps: Discover a mutation engine or generic adapter.
Select changed and critical mutation scope.
Run mutants through Hub with time budgets.
Classify killed, survived, timeout and equivalent mutants.
Enforce mutation-score policy and create findings.
acceptance: Timeout does not automatically count as killed.
Surviving critical mutants block.
Equivalent-mutant waivers need review.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Weak test detected, strengthened and remeasured.

issues.title: Implement InvariantReviewAgent
issues.problem: Domain and architecture invariants need explicit, executable review.
steps: Extract invariants from ACs, schemas and architecture docs.
Assign stable invariant IDs.
Map each invariant to state transitions and tests.
Execute positive and negative checks.
Require independent sign-off.
acceptance: Every critical invariant has executable proof.
Contradictory invariants block planning.
Review prose alone is not a PASS.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Intentional invariant violation fixtures.

issues.title: Implement ConcurrencyReliabilityAgent
issues.problem: Races, deadlocks, starvation, ordering and leaks often evade deterministic suites.
steps: Detect concurrent surfaces.
Create controlled schedules, seeds and repetitions.
Test cancellation and contention storms.
Measure deadlocks, starvation and resource leaks.
Persist replay data and traces.
acceptance: Failures are reproducible or honestly marked nondeterministic.
Budgets and repetitions are recorded.
No leak or hung process is ignored.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Race, deadlock, ordering and leak fixtures.

issues.title: Implement FaultInjectionRecoveryAgent
issues.problem: Recovery, retries and idempotency must survive real partial failures.
steps: Define a fault matrix by dependency.
Inject timeout, network loss, rate limit, process kill, disk full and corrupt response.
Observe retry, compensation and idempotency.
Verify no duplicate external effect.
Capture recovery timeline.
acceptance: Each critical dependency has failure coverage.
Partial failure never yields PASS without recovery proof.
Duplicate effect is a blocking defect.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Fault matrix with success, exhausted and compensation cases.

issues.title: Implement FlakyRepeatabilityAgent and quarantine rules
issues.problem: Intermittent tests cannot be hidden by retries.
steps: Fingerprint test failures.
Repeat under controlled seed/environment.
Estimate instability with raw attempts.
Separate deterministic failure from flaky behavior.
Define quarantine that never releases a critical gate.
acceptance: A retry pass does not erase earlier failure.
Flaky status remains non-passing.
History and confidence inputs are auditable.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Known flaky and deterministic fixtures.

issues.title: Implement ApplicationSecurityAgent
issues.problem: Security-critical paths need SAST, dynamic and abuse-case validation.
steps: Discover SAST/DAST/secrets and language-specific checks.
Map threat surfaces from impact.
Test injection, traversal, authn/authz, permissions and unsafe I/O.
Redact sensitive evidence.
Normalize severity and blocking policy.
acceptance: Critical/high findings block by policy.
Secrets never appear in persisted output.
Security agent remains independent of author.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Vulnerable corpus and SARIF golden output.

issues.title: Implement SupplyChainSecurityAgent
issues.problem: Dependencies and release artifacts can be vulnerable or untraceable.
steps: Validate lockfiles and registry sources.
Scan vulnerabilities and licenses.
Detect dependency confusion/typosquatting risk.
Generate/verify SBOM, checksum and provenance.
Bind results to exact artifacts.
acceptance: Critical dependency or untraceable artifact blocks.
Present-but-invalid SBOM/provenance fails.
Allowed license policy is explicit.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Malicious dependency and tampered artifact fixtures.

issues.title: Implement statistically defensible PerformanceBenchmarkAgent
issues.problem: Performance claims need real baselines and repeatable statistics.
steps: Detect hot paths and native harness.
Record controlled hardware/software environment.
Warm up and run at least ten repetitions when reproducible.
Store raw samples, p50/p95/p99, variance and confidence interval.
Compare against a commit-bound baseline and budget.
acceptance: No estimate or unavailable metric is reported as saving.
Regression verdict follows declared statistical policy.
Raw samples and baseline are retained.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Stable/noisy/regressed benchmark fixtures.

issues.title: Implement LoadStressSoakAgent
issues.problem: Load, saturation and long-running leaks require separate evidence.
steps: Define load, spike, stress and soak profiles.
Request bounded resources from Hub.
Measure throughput, latency, CPU, RSS, disk, network and handles.
Find saturation and recovery point.
Detect long-run resource growth.
acceptance: Profiles and duration are policy-controlled.
Machine overload guardrails are enforced by Hub.
Leak/saturation findings include raw time series.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Load curve, saturation and seeded leak fixtures.

issues.title: Implement CompatibilityMigrationAgent
issues.problem: OS/runtime/version and data migrations can fail outside the developer environment.
steps: Resolve supported compatibility matrix.
Test N-1/current/next where policy requires.
Execute install, upgrade, downgrade and rollback.
Validate schema/data migrations and idempotency.
Record unsupported cells as explicit policy decisions.
acceptance: Every supported cell has evidence.
Failed migration preserves recovery/rollback proof.
Compatibility is not inferred from compilation.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Version matrix and rollback fixtures.

issues.title: Implement a generic command/container quality profile for any project
issues.problem: Unknown toolchains still need full quality without extension code changes.
steps: Define declarative commands, dependencies, result parsers and artifacts.
Support containerized hermetic execution through Loop.
Map generic outputs to lanes and evidence.
Validate paths, env and resource requests.
Ship an onboarding generator and schema.
acceptance: An unrecognized project can integrate all gates through configuration.
Commands still execute only through Hub.
Unknown result formats block.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Two synthetic unknown toolchains and one container profile.

issues.title: Implement first-party Python quality adapters
issues.problem: Python projects need native, async and packaging-aware coverage.
steps: Add pytest/unittest, coverage, Hypothesis/fuzz, mutation, lint/type/security adapters.
Support sync/async, namespace packages and monorepos.
Support wheel/sdist clean-install smoke.
Normalize JUnit, coverage and SARIF.
Pin and report tool versions.
acceptance: Representative Python projects exercise every supported lane.
No hidden dependency on source checkout.
Missing optional tool is explicit.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Sync, async, CLI, library and monorepo fixtures.

issues.title: Implement first-party Rust quality adapters
issues.problem: Rust workspaces need features, targets and native quality tools.
steps: Add cargo test/nextest, llvm-cov, clippy, proptest/fuzz, mutation and audit adapters.
Handle workspaces, features and target triples.
Test crate packaging and installable binaries.
Capture sanitizer/Miri evidence when policy requires.
Normalize cargo outputs.
acceptance: Workspace and feature matrix is supported.
Crate artifact smoke is real.
Unsafe/concurrency checks are representable.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Workspace, feature and binary crate fixtures.

issues.title: Implement first-party JavaScript and TypeScript adapters
issues.problem: Node projects vary across ESM/CJS, runners, package managers and monorepos.
steps: Add Jest/Vitest/node:test, Istanbul, fast-check, mutation, lint/type and audit adapters.
Support npm/pnpm/yarn lockfiles and workspaces.
Handle ESM/CJS and browser/server splits.
Test packed tarball install and bin entrypoints.
Normalize JUnit, LCOV and SARIF.
acceptance: No package-manager assumption is silent.
Packed artifact is tested.
Browser and Node surfaces remain distinct.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
ESM, CJS, frontend, backend and monorepo fixtures.

issues.title: Implement first-party .NET quality adapters
issues.problem: .NET solutions need multi-project discovery, Coverlet, analyzers and package smoke.
steps: Add dotnet test, TRX, Coverlet, analyzers, FsCheck, mutation and NuGet audit adapters.
Resolve solution/project/test dependency graph.
Support async, AOT and target-framework matrices.
Pack/install NuGet and CLI artifacts.
Normalize coverage and diagnostics.
acceptance: Multi-project solutions and TFMs are handled.
TRX/coverage bindings are exact.
Published package smoke avoids source references.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Library, web API, worker and multi-TFM fixtures.

issues.title: Implement first-party Go quality adapters
issues.problem: Go modules need race, fuzz, coverage and binary/package checks.
steps: Add go test, cover, race, fuzz, vet/staticcheck and vulnerability adapters.
Handle modules, workspaces, build tags and CGO.
Test built binaries in clean environments.
Normalize JSON test events and coverage.
Capture seeds and race reports.
acceptance: Race/fuzz failures are replayable.
Build-tag matrix is explicit.
Module cache does not hide missing dependencies.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Module, workspace, CGO and CLI fixtures.

issues.title: Implement first-party Java and Kotlin adapters
issues.problem: JVM builds need Gradle/Maven, test engines, coverage, mutation and packaging support.
steps: Add JUnit/TestNG, JaCoCo, PIT, static/security and property-test adapters.
Support Maven/Gradle multi-module builds.
Handle Java/Kotlin and supported JVM matrices.
Test jar/application artifacts outside checkout.
Normalize XML reports and diagnostics.
acceptance: Build-tool choice is detected, not guessed.
Multi-module coverage is correct.
Packaged application smoke is real.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Maven, Gradle, Java, Kotlin and multi-module fixtures.

issues.title: Implement first-party native C and C++ adapters
issues.problem: C and C++ projects need build-system, sanitizer, fuzz and ABI-aware testing.
steps: Support CMake/CTest, Meson and common native runners.
Integrate coverage, sanitizers, fuzzing and static analysis.
Capture compiler/target/architecture identity.
Test produced libraries/binaries/packages.
Model platform-specific N/A with approval.
acceptance: Memory/UB sanitizer failures block.
Architecture matrix is explicit.
Built binaries are tested outside build tree.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
CMake/Meson C and C++ library, binary, ABI and sanitizer fixtures.

issues.title: Implement first-party PHP adapters
issues.problem: PHP projects need native test, coverage, static and Composer package checks.
steps: Add PHPUnit and Pest adapters.
Add coverage, static analysis, mutation and dependency security.
Support Composer lockfiles and common PHP frameworks.
Test packaged/library/CLI entrypoints.
Normalize test and coverage reports.
acceptance: Framework detection is evidence-based.
Dependency installation is hermetic.
Package smoke avoids developer-global state.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
PHP library, web, framework and CLI fixtures.

issues.title: Implement AI, LLM and agent-system quality profile
issues.problem: Nondeterministic agents require evals beyond exact text assertions.
steps: Model task success, tool-use, structured output and safety criteria.
Add prompt-injection and hallucinated-success adversarial cases.
Run repeated samples with seeds/model/provider metadata.
Measure latency, tokens and cost only when directly observed.
Use statistical thresholds and regression datasets.
acceptance: No exact-text-only gate for semantic tasks.
Hallucinated tool success is detected.
Missing token/cost metrics are null with reason.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Fake providers, tool misuse and adversarial prompt corpus.

issues.title: Implement database, data-pipeline and migration quality profile
issues.problem: Data changes need correctness, rollback, volume and consistency assurance.
steps: Model schema, migration and data-quality contracts.
Test forward/backward migration and idempotency.
Validate constraints, transactions and partial failure.
Run representative volume/performance tests.
Audit backup/restore or compensation path.
acceptance: No destructive migration passes without rollback evidence.
Data invariants survive partial failure.
Volume baseline is measured.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Relational, document and pipeline migration fixtures.

issues.title: Implement web UI and visual-regression quality profile
issues.problem: UI correctness includes real browser interaction and controlled visual regressions.
steps: Discover routes and supported browsers.
Execute real browser interactions through Loop-managed tooling.
Audit console errors, network failures and browser runtime errors.
Capture screenshots/video and visual diffs.
Separate approved visual updates from regressions.
acceptance: Critical flows have browser E2E evidence.
Console, network and page runtime errors follow policy.
Visual baseline changes require review.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Responsive interaction, browser-error and seeded visual regression fixtures.

issues.title: Implement container-image and Kubernetes quality profile
issues.problem: Container images and Kubernetes resources need security, deployability and rollback validation.
steps: Validate Dockerfiles/images, Kubernetes manifests and Helm charts.
Scan configuration, secrets, vulnerabilities and privileges.
Run disposable deployment smoke where authorized.
Verify health, resource limits and rollback.
Bind evidence to immutable image/plan digests.
acceptance: No live production mutation occurs in tests.
Privileged/insecure configs block.
Rollback and cleanup are proven.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Container-image, Kubernetes and Helm fixture projects.

issues.title: Integrate GitHub Checks, PR annotations and issue lifecycle
issues.problem: Quality results must mechanically block stale PRs and issue closure.
steps: Publish one idempotent GitHub Check per quality run.
Attach annotations and evidence links.
Bind checks to exact head SHA.
Reject rerun/stale commit confusion.
Let Loop delivery close/merge only after accepted gate.
acceptance: Quality extension never closes directly.
Failed/stale checks block merge and closure.
Permission failures are visible BLOCKED states.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Fork, rerun, stale SHA and insufficient-permission cases.

issues.title: Provide CI-neutral CLI, exit codes and standard report formats
issues.problem: The engine must work outside GitHub and integrate with any CI.
steps: Freeze CLI commands and stable exit-code taxonomy.
Emit JSON, JUnit, SARIF, Cobertura/LCOV and Markdown.
Stream progress without corrupting machine output.
Support artifact size limits and path portability.
Publish reusable CI examples without coupling core logic.
acceptance: Any CI can invoke plan/run/gate/report.
Formats validate against schemas.
Oversized/missing artifacts fail predictably.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Linux/macOS/Windows path and format fixtures.

issues.title: Add correlated observability and historical trend comparison
issues.problem: Operators need per-lane visibility and trends without overwriting raw evidence.
steps: Emit structured logs, metrics and traces with Loop IDs.
Measure queue wait, execution, retries and gate latency.
Persist immutable raw samples and derived trends separately.
Redact secrets and PII.
Compare baselines by compatible environment fingerprints.
acceptance: Every stage is correlatable end to end.
Interrupted traces remain diagnosable.
Incompatible baselines are not compared.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Redaction, missing metrics and interrupted trace cases.

issues.title: Validate the complete Simplicio ecosystem integration matrix
issues.problem: Quality must work with isolated and combined Simplicio components.
steps: Test loop+quality alone.
Test runtime, mapper, dev-cli, agent and code combinations.
Test optional components absent and incompatible.
Run install/upgrade matrices from published artifacts.
Compare receipts and degraded behavior.
acceptance: Supported combinations are explicitly green.
Optional absence never fabricates integration.
Version incompatibility fails fast.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Clean environment matrix for all declared combinations.

issues.title: Create golden defect projects and self-dogfood conformance
issues.problem: Agents need proof that they find real defects and avoid false PASS.
steps: Create small projects with one seeded defect per agent/language.
Record expected findings and evidence.
Run the full extension against every fixture.
Run the extension against its own repository.
Track false positives, misses and runtime.
acceptance: Every agent catches its seeded defect.
Clean fixtures do not produce false blockers.
Dogfood is required before release.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Golden corpus versioned with expected outputs.

issues.title: Publish complete documentation and operational runbooks
issues.problem: Users need reproducible setup, extension and recovery guidance.
steps: Document architecture boundary, policies, agents and schemas.
Document CLI, CI, reports and evidence verification.
Publish adapter authoring tutorial.
Write troubleshooting, recovery, cancellation and waiver runbooks.
Validate docs from a clean environment.
acceptance: A third party can onboard an unknown project.
All commands are tested.
No duplicated or stale canonical contract.
tests: Documentation command audit and clean tutorial reproduction.

issues.title: Build a secure, reproducible release-candidate process
issues.problem: A quality tool must prove its own published artifacts.
steps: Adopt one canonical version and build once.
Create reproducible wheel/sdist, checksums, SBOM and provenance.
Sign/attest artifacts with short-lived identity.
Install published artifacts in clean environments.
Run golden suite, canary, upgrade and rollback before candidate promotion.
acceptance: Candidate bytes match tested bytes.
No static publishing secret is required where OIDC exists.
Rollback is documented and tested.
Candidate promotion remains blocked unless dogfood and ecosystem matrix pass.
tests: Test with unit, integration, system, regression, real fixtures, >=90% changed branch coverage, and attach commit-bound evidence.
Tampered artifact, partial publish, clean install and rollback fault cases.

issues.title: Implement first-party Swift quality adapters
issues.problem: Swift packages and applications need platform, concurrency and XCTest-aware validation independent of C/C++ tooling.
steps: Add SwiftPM, XCTest, coverage, lint and static-analysis adapters.
Support libraries, executables, async tests and package plugins.
Capture Swift, SDK, target, architecture and platform identity.
Test built products outside the source/build tree.
Normalize results and platform-specific N/A decisions.
acceptance: Supported Swift package forms produce normalized evidence.
Async/concurrency and strict-concurrency failures block.
Platform exclusions require explicit approved N/A.
tests: Library, executable, async, actor-isolation and package-plugin fixtures.
Clean artifact smoke on each supported platform cell.

issues.title: Implement first-party Ruby quality adapters
issues.problem: Ruby libraries, CLIs and web applications need RSpec/Minitest, coverage, mutation and Bundler-aware validation.
steps: Add RSpec, Minitest, coverage, lint/type and mutation adapters.
Support Bundler lockfiles, gems and common Ruby web frameworks.
Model Ruby engine/version and native-extension compatibility.
Test gem and CLI artifacts without developer-global state.
Normalize test, coverage, security and static findings.
acceptance: Framework discovery is evidence-based and deterministic.
Gem smoke uses the exact built artifact.
Missing Bundler or required analyzer never appears clean.
tests: RSpec, Minitest, gem, CLI, Rails-style and native-extension fixtures.
Lockfile drift and developer-global dependency negative cases.

issues.title: Implement infrastructure-as-code quality profile
issues.problem: Terraform, OpenTofu, Pulumi and configuration plans need policy, drift, security and rollback proof without touching production.
steps: Discover IaC engines, modules, state backends and policy tools.
Run formatting, validation, static security and plan checks through Hub.
Use disposable test accounts/emulators only when explicitly authorized.
Analyze plan changes, destructive actions, drift and secret exposure.
Verify rollback, cleanup and immutable plan digests.
acceptance: No production apply occurs during quality validation.
Destructive or privileged plan changes follow explicit policy.
Plan, policy, state and environment identities are auditable.
tests: Terraform/OpenTofu/Pulumi module and policy fixtures.
Seeded destructive plan, secret, drift and cleanup-failure cases.

issues.title: Implement accessibility quality profile
issues.problem: Automated and interaction-level accessibility needs its own policy, evidence and manual-review boundary.
steps: Discover applicable WCAG target, supported browsers and assistive paths.
Run semantic, keyboard, focus, contrast and accessible-name audits.
Exercise critical flows with keyboard-only interaction.
Record automated coverage limits and required manual review explicitly.
Normalize violations by rule, severity, element and user impact.
acceptance: Critical WCAG violations block according to policy.
Automated PASS never claims full manual accessibility coverage.
Waivers identify rule, scope, expiry and independent approver.
tests: Seeded name, contrast, focus, landmark and keyboard-trap fixtures.
False-positive, waiver-expiry and browser-parity cases.

issues.title: Implement ImplementationCompletenessAgent
issues.problem: Green tests can coexist with placeholders, stubs, TODO-only paths, mocks in production and unimplemented acceptance criteria.
steps: Trace each acceptance criterion to production behavior and evidence.
Detect placeholders, pass-through stubs, disabled branches and panic/not-implemented paths.
Distinguish legitimate abstract/test seams from incomplete production code.
Exercise every claimed entrypoint using real artifacts.
Emit blocking findings without implementing product features locally.
acceptance: No acceptance criterion is satisfied only by a mock or placeholder.
Intentional TODOs require scoped policy and do not cover required behavior.
Findings route to Loop implementation/recovery, not a local fix loop.
tests: Seeded stub, constant-return, TODO, disabled-feature and mock-in-production fixtures.
Legitimate abstract method and test-double false-positive cases.

issues.title: Implement IndependentCodeReviewAgent
issues.problem: Tests do not replace an independent review of correctness, architecture, maintainability and unintended behavior.
steps: Review the exact diff plus impacted context and public contracts.
Check correctness, error handling, invariants, compatibility and maintainability.
Separate blocking defects from evidence-backed nonblocking suggestions.
Forbid author/executor self-approval and record reviewer identity.
Bind review findings and resolution evidence to the exact source SHA.
acceptance: Reviewer identity is independent and ledger-backed.
Every blocking finding is resolved or explicitly blocks completion.
Review does not claim runtime proof it did not execute.
tests: Seeded correctness, compatibility, error-swallowing and architecture defects.
Self-review, stale-diff and unsupported-claim negative cases.

issues.title: Implement TestSelectionValidationAgent
issues.problem: Incremental test selection can be fast but silently omit tests that a full suite would fail.
steps: Record why each selected and omitted test relates to the impact map.
Compare incremental selection with scheduled full-suite reference runs.
Measure false-negative, false-positive and runtime tradeoffs.
Invalidate selection caches on graph, config or toolchain changes.
Escalate to full suite when confidence or risk falls below policy.
acceptance: Critical changes cannot rely on unvalidated selection.
A missed failing test is a blocking selector defect.
Selection savings are reported only with full-suite comparison evidence.
tests: Seeded indirect dependency and stale-cache false-negative fixtures.
Full-suite parity, rename/delete and monorepo boundary cases.

issues.title: Implement OperationalReadinessAgent
issues.problem: A project can pass functional tests yet be impossible to operate because health, logs, metrics, traces, alerts or failure messages are absent or misleading.
steps: Discover declared health/readiness and observability contracts.
Exercise startup, degraded dependency, overload and shutdown behavior.
Verify logs, metrics and traces correlate without leaking secrets or PII.
Test alert/runbook signals and actionable error messages where declared.
Emit explicit N/A when a project has no operational surface.
acceptance: Services prove health/readiness semantics under real failure conditions.
Critical failures are diagnosable from emitted signals.
Telemetry existence alone is not treated as correctness.
tests: Seeded false-health, missing-correlation, secret-log and silent-failure fixtures.
Startup, dependency outage, overload, cancellation and clean-shutdown cases.

issues.title: Implement PrivacyComplianceAgent
issues.problem: Projects handling personal data need executable checks for minimization, consent, retention, export, deletion and data leakage.
steps: Discover declared data classes, jurisdictions and privacy policy inputs.
Trace PII through storage, logs, telemetry, caches and third parties.
Test consent, access/export, correction, deletion and retention workflows.
Validate redaction, minimization and non-production fixture generation.
Require expert approval for legal interpretations outside executable facts.
acceptance: No synthetic test writes real personal data to external systems.
Executable privacy promises have commit-bound evidence.
Legal uncertainty is BLOCKED/needs review, never fabricated PASS.
tests: Seeded PII log, missing deletion, over-retention and consent-bypass fixtures.
Synthetic data, jurisdiction policy and expert-review boundary cases.

issues.title: Implement DocumentationQualityAgent
issues.problem: Project documentation can describe commands, APIs and examples that no longer work even when code tests pass.
steps: Discover user-facing docs, examples, snippets, links and generated references.
Execute commands and code examples in clean controlled environments.
Validate links, API symbols, versions and configuration names.
Compare generated reference docs with current public contracts.
Separate executable proof from editorial/manual review.
acceptance: All declared executable examples are tested or explicitly scoped N/A.
Broken links, commands and stale public symbols produce findings.
Documentation tests do not mutate production services.
tests: Seeded stale command, broken link, wrong version and invalid snippet fixtures.
Clean-room tutorial reproduction and generated-reference drift cases.

issues.title: Certify the expanded universal quality layer for GA
issues.problem: The added ecosystems and assurance agents need the same golden-defect, clean-fixture and release proof as the original scope.
steps: Add one seeded-defect and one clean fixture per newly added agent/profile.
Run the entire strict matrix through the authoritative Loop path.
Measure misses, false blockers, flakiness, runtime and resource use.
Rebuild and install the exact release candidate in clean environments.
Publish the compatibility matrix, evidence index and signed release decision.
acceptance: Every new agent catches its seeded defect and accepts its clean control.
No lane is unmapped across policy, agent graph and manifest.
GA remains blocked without fresh Oracle-authorized end-to-end evidence.
tests: Expanded golden corpus and full cross-profile dogfood run.
Tampered, stale, self-approved and omitted-lane release blockers.
